name: Oostburg State Bank
description_short: Independent community bank serving Sheboygan County since 1908, offering personal banking, business financing, mortgages, and wealth management with local decision-making.
description_long:
Oostburg State Bank was founded in 1908 and has operated as a fiercely independent, locally owned bank throughout its 118-year history in Sheboygan County, Wisconsin. The bank maintains three physical locations in Oostburg, Cedar Grove, and Sheboygan Falls, with leadership including CEO Eric Glewen who emphasizes the bank's commitment to local community banking principles.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including personal banking (free checking, savings accounts, retirement planning, home loans), business banking solutions for startups and expansion, mortgage lending, and wealth/protection services. They provide modern banking tools alongside personalized service, positioning themselves as an alternative to national bank chains. The bank maintains ATM access, online banking, and various deposit products with published rate information available to customers.

Oostburg State Bank distinguishes itself through local decision-making authority, with loan and service decisions made by community members who understand regional customer needs rather than distant corporate offices. Their CEO message and website emphasize relationship-based banking where customers interact with real people from the community. The bank is FDIC-insured, maintains federal compliance (Routing #075906346, NMLS #416680), and operates extended hours including Saturday lobby service in two locations.

As a regional community bank, Oostburg State Bank is best suited for customers prioritizing local service relationships and community investment over national bank convenience. The primary limitation is geographic restriction to Sheboygan County, Wisconsin, making services unavailable to customers outside this service area. Customers seeking extensive branch networks or specialized financial products available only at large national institutions may find limited options here. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Home loan origination and mortgage lending
Business banking and financing for startups and expansion
Safe deposit boxes implied through full-service banking
pros: Locally owned and independent with 118 years of continuous operation in Sheboygan County
Free checking account with no hidden fees, explicitly branded as 'actually free'
Local decision-making by community-based bankers who understand regional needs
Extended Saturday lobby hours (8:30 AM - 11:30 AM) at Oostburg and Sheboygan Falls locations
Full FDIC insurance protection and federal compliance (Member FDIC, NMLS #416680)
Comprehensive service offerings including personal, business, mortgage, and wealth management
Published deposit rates and fee schedules available transparently on website
cons: Geographic limitation to three locations in Sheboygan County, Wisconsin with no online-only banking option mentioned
Limited to regional market means fewer ATM locations compared to national banks
No mention of advanced digital banking features, mobile app functionality, or cutting-edge fintech offerings
Business hours exclude Sunday service at all locations, limiting accessibility for weekend banking needs
No Saturday drive-thru service at Cedar Grove location creates unequal service across branches
best_for: Sheboygan County residents seeking personalized banking relationships with local decision-makers
Small business owners and startups needing accessible business financing from community lenders
Customers who prioritize supporting independent, locally-owned financial institutions over national chains
Homebuyers and homeowners in the region seeking mortgage services with local loan officers
diagnosis: Oostburg State Bank is ideal for Sheboygan County residents and businesses prioritizing relationship-based banking with local decision-making authority. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation to three Wisconsin locations, making services inaccessible to customers outside the immediate service area.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Independent community bank serving Sheboygan County since 1908 with personal banking and wealth management.
